reason: The statement does not apply because Saul, not an evil king, was chosen by the LORD to be king over Israel. While Saul did disobey the command to utterly destroy the Amalekites, it was not due to oppressing the Hebrews in captivity but rather sparing some of the spoils. The text depicts Saul's actions as disobedience to the specific command of the LORD rather than oppression of the Hebrews.
